fundamentals.name: Snowflake Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Snowflake Inc. provides a cloud-based data platform for various organizations in the United States and internationally. The company's platform includes artificial intelligence (AI) Data Cloud, which enables customers to consolidate data into a single source of truth to drive meaningful business insights, build data applications, and share data and data products, as well as applies AI for solving business problems. It serves financial services, advertising, media and entertainment, retail and consumer goods, healthcare and life sciences, manufacturing, technology, telecom, travel and hospitality, and government and defense industries, as well as the public sector. the company has a collaboration with OpenAI, L.L.C. for the development of AI solutions for joint enterprise customers that deliver tangible return on investment. The company was formerly known as Snowflake Computing, Inc. and changed its name to Snowflake Inc. in April 2019. Snowflake Inc. was incorporated in 2012 and is based in Menlo Park, California.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable and trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 21.9%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 33.5% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow (TTM) $1.67B — positive cash generation.
Analyst growth est, current FY: 53.5% — strong future growth expected.
Consensus: strong_buy (47 analysts) — broad analyst support.
negatives: Operating margin -22.2% and Profit margin -23.8% — currently loss-making.
Return on equity -54.9% and Return on assets -10.5% — negative returns.
Debt/Equity: 142.91 — high leverage relative to equity.

news.paragraph: Snowflake stock is surging following a strong Q1 earnings report that beat estimates, driven by AI tailwinds and a significant $6 billion deal with a major cloud provider. The company's revenue growth of 33.5% year-over-year and a substantial beat on Q1 EPS are fueling optimism, with analysts maintaining a 'strong buy' consensus and a mean target of $277.03. Despite a high forward P/E, the stock's recent performance indicates positive momentum.
